segments.text: And the Lord said to Moses,
Speak to the priests, the sons of Aaron, and say to them: 'None shall defile himself for the dead among his people,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: except for his relatives who are nearest to him: his mother, his father, his son, his daughter, and his brother;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: also his virgin sister who is near to him, who has had no husband, for her he may defile himself.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Otherwise he shall not defile himself, being a chief man among his people, to profane himself.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: They shall not make any bald place on their heads, nor shall they shave the edges of their beards nor make any cuttings in their flesh.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: They shall be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God, for they offer the offerings of the Lord made by fire, and the bread of their God; therefore they shall be holy.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: They shall not take a wife who is a harlot or a defiled woman, nor shall they take a woman divorced from her husband; for the priest is holy to his God.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Therefore you shall consecrate him, for he offers the bread of your God. He shall be holy to you, for I the Lord, who sanctify you, am holy.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The daughter of any priest, if she profanes herself by playing the harlot, she profanes her father. She shall be burned with fire.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He who is the high priest among his brethren, on whose head the anointing oil was poured and who is consecrated to wear the garments, shall not uncover his head nor tear his clothes;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: nor shall he go near any dead body, nor defile himself for his father or his mother;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: nor shall he go out of the sanctuary, nor profane the sanctuary of his God; for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And he shall take a wife in her virginity.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: A widow or a divorced woman or a defiled woman or a harlot—these he shall not marry; but he shall take a virgin of his own people as wife.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Nor shall he profane his posterity among his people, for I the Lord sanctify him.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to Aaron, saying: 'No man of your descendants in succeeding generations, who has any defect, may approach to offer the bread of his God.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: For any man who has a defect shall not approach: a man blind or lame, who has a marred face or any limb too long,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: a man who has a broken foot or broken hand,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: or is a hunchback or a dwarf, or a man who has a defect in his eye, or eczema or scab, or is a eunuch.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: No man of the descendants of Aaron the priest, who has a defect, shall come near to offer the offerings made by fire to the Lord. He has a defect; he shall not come near to offer the bread of his God.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He may eat the bread of his God, both the most holy and the holy;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: only he shall not go near the veil or approach the altar, because he has a defect, lest he profane My sanctuaries; for I the Lord sanctify them.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And Moses told it to Aaron and his sons, and to all the children of Israel.
